summary:
Unmarried men have a significantly higher risk of dying from accidents than unmarried women, indicating stronger gender-based variation in mortality linked to marital status. Social isolation associated with being single also correlates with poorer health outcomes for both genders but manifests more severely among males regarding fatal incidents. 

Note: The summary above contains two complete sentences focusing only on clinically significant findings related directly to the query about variations based on gender.
